body: @pixelastic I would like your input on this one :)
I would prefer a span for the icon (and template for icon class and/or content). That should help css to use font icons easier. ``
^ updated with this, very nice

I'm ok with creating this widget, this is definitely needed.
1. I don't think the wrapping `<div class="ais-clear-all--item">` is needed. The `--item` class is used when there is several elements (like for a refinement list). When there is only one button, I would get rid of it.
2. I would not add a `span` or a `i` for the icon, and let the `theme.css` handle that (using `:before`). If people really want to add an icon, they could add it in a custom template. We need to have the bare minimum markup to make the `default.css` theme usable, no more.
3. I'd rather pu a `a` than a `button` (we already have `a` everywhere)
4. Not sure we need the header/footer for that one.

> I don't think the wrapping <div class="ais-clear-all--item"> is needed. The --item class is used when there is several elements (like for a refinement list). When there is only one button, I would get rid of it.

I also thought the same but toggle has label + item and found that maybe we should do the same here?

> I'd rather pu a a than a button (we already have a everywhere)

Indeed, passing a `<a>` to a button is only some css while passing a button to an `<a>` is tricky.

> Not sure we need the header/footer for that one.

Since it could be located on the left top refinement list, maybe we still need it. Also more consistent to have it everywhere (but the hits).

> I would not add a span or a i for the icon, and let the theme.css handle that (using :before). If people really want to add an icon, they could add it in a custom template. We need to have the bare minimum markup to make the default.css theme usable, no more.

Not sure about your proposition

Could we add an option to be able to clear:
- Refinements only
- Query only
- Refinements and Query

> Query only

Not sure about clearing the query only
If we are talking about clearing the query, I may have an idea for the search box :)

> If we are talking about clearing the query, I may have an idea for the search box :)

Yes clearing only the query seems like a searchBox option

> I also thought the same but toggle has label + item and found that maybe we should do the same here?

Toggle has an item wrapper because it internally uses the `refinementList` component (but only display one item). I'd rather we fix it in toggle than adding it in this widget :)

Makes sense to keep the header/footer, I agree.

Regarding the `span`/`i` for the icon: I think the icon is a secondary concern for this widget. It is not needed to make the widget work, it is only styling. So I would not add any markup for it in the default template. Like we would no add a `strong` node around active element in default template, we should not add a `span` to denote a possible icon in the default template. This can be handled through either CSS or custom templates.

> Like we would no add a strong node around active element in default template, we should not add a span to denote a possible icon in the default template. This can be handled through either CSS or custom templates.

Ok right makes sense

> Toggle has an item wrapper because it internally uses the refinementList component (but only display one item). I'd rather we fix it in toggle than adding it in this widget :)

Hm I think it did not have one at first and then we made it so for consistency (https://github.com/algolia/instantsearch.js/commit/498bad6a299b9997a3fe12985b52b44e246c5fde). We had the discussion together I believe.

> Like we would no add a strong node around active element in default template, we should not add a span to denote a possible icon in the default template. This can be handled through either CSS or custom templates.
> Ok right makes sense

On the other hand, if by default there's a cross, should it be in default.css or in the default template? (much like the css/input checkbox discussion we had).

> Hm I think it did not have one at first and then we made it so for consistency (498bad6). We had the discussion together I believe.

In my head the consistency was more about naming it `item` instead of `body`. If there is to be a list of one `item`, let it at least be named `item`. I can understand there is some display consistency between a `toggle` and a `refinementList`, so using the same markup makes sense. But for a `clearAll`, I would go without the superfluous `item` here.

> On the other hand, if by default there's a cross, should it be in default.css or in the default template? (much like the css/input checkbox discussion we had).

There's a bit of déjà-vu here :). I also think the icon is not mandatory for the widget to work, nor for the user to understand how it works. A simple link/button with "Remove all filters" is more explicit than the icon. So I would put it in the themes.

> There's a bit of déjà-vu here :). I also think the icon is not mandatory for the widget to work, nor for the user to understand how it works. A simple link/button with "Remove all filters" is more explicit than the icon. So I would put it in the themes.

WFM
@pixelastic I updated the widget proposal, let me know

WFM regarding the markup.

But I do not understand the difference between `disableWhenNoRefinements` and `hideContainerWhenNoRefinements`.
Yep sorry, updated
Looks good :+1:
I think that widget should be merged with #112 -> it's very similar & you generally have that "clear all" button next to the active refinements..

> I think that widget should be merged with #112 -> it's very similar & you generally have that "clear all" button next to the active refinements..

Not against it, we only need a precise api proposal for this one and then we are good
Oh I would rename `clearAll` to `clearAllRefinedValues` to make it more clear (and fit the other widget's naming).

@redox :+1:, making it clearer

Still not sure we need to wrap the link in an `item` element, but WFM anyway.
Is this issue still the one we are working on? The API proposal seems outdated.
If you have a final widget proposal, best option is to reopen a new issue and close this one to avoid confusions
